ksa_text: Knowledge of data catalog and metadata management platforms, including active metadata principles, automated lineage tracking, and business glossary governance.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DG-K-004 (DG, Knowledge) does not clear the moderate fit inclusion threshold of 0.43. Composite score 0.4224 is insufficient to establish relevance to health informatics, clinical data analysis, and healthcare BI at IC-to-senior-IC level. Framework signals (nice=0.422, dcwf=0.410, ddat=0.407) do not demonstrate sufficient alignment with core analytics/BI work.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 exclude upheld. Score 0.4224 below threshold for moderate fit domain; no rescuing analytics text signals. No internal inconsistencies identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does DG-domain KSA DG-K-004 (composite 0.4224) appear in published data analyst or BI role profiles?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Composite 0.4224 for DG domain does not meet the evidentiary bar for ANL IC-level inclusion per DAMA DMBOK governance competencies; governance awareness at practitioner level appears in O*NET 15-2051 expanded skill sets. External sources confirm this competency is not a standard expectation for Health Informatics Specialist at the targeted seniority band.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 exclude upheld. External evidence confirms DG domain KSA at composite 0.4224 is not a standard requirement for Health Informatics Specialist at IC-to-senior-IC level.

decision_rationale: Pipeline consensus: exclude. P2 exclude confirmed by P3 (internal) and P4 (external) adversarial agents. Zero overturns. Composite 0.4224, domain DG, scope moderate fit. High confidence.
